items.title: Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman calls March 25, 1971 genocide as ‘pre-planned massacre’
items.summary: “The Great Independence Day inspires us anew with the spirit of courage, sacrifice and patriotism. The fundamental goal of independence was to establish a Bangladesh that is free from discrimination, and to be democratic, peaceful and prosperous,” Tarique Rahman said

items.title: Trump to visit China in May after rescheduling trip due to Iran war
items.summary: Asked if the war would conclude by the time of the meeting in May, the White House Press Secretary said, “Again, as I've said, we've always estimated approximately four to six weeks.”

items.title: Panel wants prosecution of ousted Nepal PM Oli over violence in Gen Z protests
items.summary: The ⁠report comes two days before rapper-turned politician Balendra Shah is sworn in as the new prime minister after winning a landslide in the ‌parliamentary election following the protests

items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said that Iran was taking part in peace talks but suggested Tehran’s denials were because Iranian negotiators fear being killed by their own side; Iran has set its own conditions for a ceasefire, including compensation for war damages

items.title: North Korea, Belarus sign ‘friendship’ treaty during Lukashenko visit
items.summary: Besides supporting Russia’s war against Ukraine — around 2,000 North Korean soldiers are thought to have died — both nations are under Western sanctions and are accused of gross human rights violations

items.title: Iran, U.S. harden their positions as Tehran keeps its grip on Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Tehran moved to formalise its control over the crucial Strait of Hormuz while Washington prepared for the arrival of U.S. combat forces in the region that could be used on the ground

items.summary: Global oil and gas prices have surged after Iran virtually blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow shipping lane between the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman, that handles roughly 20% of global oil and LNG

items.summary: There were no immediate reports of any damage caused by it

items.title: U.S. to deploy 1,000 elite troops to West Asia
items.summary: In a latest addition to the Iran war effort, U.S. officials say thousands of Marines, as well as rapid response naval vessels, will be heading to the region; Iran and Israel exchange strikes; Gulf neighbours Kuwait and Saudi Arabia come under fire; Iran’s military says it also targeted a U.S. aircraft carrier

items.title: Russia says it shot down almost 400 Ukrainian drones as Moscow, Kyiv escalate aerial barrages
items.summary: Alexander Drozdenko, Governor of the Leningrad region north of Moscow, said 56 drones were shot down there, and a fire broke out in the Baltic Sea port of Ust-Luga as the result of Ukraine’s attack

items.title: Kim vows North Korea's 'unshakable' support for Russia
items.summary: Analysts say the assistance has been provided in exchange for Russia’s provision of food and weapons technology

items.title: Rights group warns of 'dystopian' Hong Kong after bookstore arrests

items.title: What is at stake at the WTO’s MC14? | Explained
items.summary: What is the context in which MC14 is taking place? What has weakened the WTO’s dispute settlement system? What are the key issues before MC14? Why is the e-commerce moratorium contentious? What role should India play at MC14?

items.title: Spanish PM says West Asia war 'far worse' than Iraq in 2003
items.summary: The Socialist premier has refused Washington’s requests to use Madrid’s military bases against Iran, despite U.S. President Donald Trump’s threat to sever trade with Spain as a result

items.summary: CCPA made it clear that only applicable taxes can be added to the price mentioned in the menu of restaurants and hotels; all input costs should be factored into the prices displayed in the menu

items.title: Iran rejects U.S. proposal, sets out conditions to end war
items.summary: The U.S. proposal offered a ceasefire and sanctions relief in return for Tehran abandoning its nuclear programme and reopening the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Iranian strikes pose ‘existential threat’, Gulf states tell UN

items.title: Most Americans say U.S. military action against Iran has gone too far, poll finds
items.summary: About 4 in 10 U.S. adults continue to approve of Mr. Trump’s performance as president, which is unchanged from last month; his approval on foreign policy, while slightly lower than his overall approval, also largely held steady

items.title: Ex-Google executive Matt Brittin named new BBC boss
items.summary: The BBC is facing a $10 billion lawsuit from Mr. Trump, who accuses the publicly funded broadcaster of defamation over how it spliced ‌together footage of parts of a speech he gave on January 6, 2021, before his supporters ‌stormed the U.S. Capitol

items.title: Iran-Israel war: A day‑by‑day rundown of the escalating crisis
items.summary: A joint U.S.-Israel attack on Iran and the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei have plunged West Asia into a fast‑spreading war, with Tehran and its allies launching retaliatory strikes across Israel, the Gulf and U.S. bases.

items.title: Sharif briefs Saudi Crown Prince as Pakistan pushes to mediate U.S.-Iran talks
items.summary: Mr. Sharif reiterated Pakistan’s strong condemnation of the recent attacks on the Kingdom and reaffirmed Islamabad's unwavering solidarity and unequivocal support for Saudi Arabia in these challenging times

items.title: New Mexico jury says Meta harms children’s mental health and safety, violating state law
items.summary: The landmark decision comes after a nearly seven-week trial, and as jurors in a federal court in California have been sequestered in deliberations for more than a week about whether Meta and YouTube should be liable in a similar case

items.title: With eyes on trade deals and reform in global governance, Lula heads to India with 260 firms

items.title: As two of the world’s largest democracies and dynamic economies, India and Brazil cannot remain distant: President Lula
items.summary: Bilateral trade between Brazil and India reached its highest level in 2025, but it remains far below its true potential, says the Brazilian President

items.title: BRICS shifts gears as Brazil passes the gavel to India amid global tensions and ambitious goals
items.summary: Brazil’s presidency of BRICS was punctuated by the aftereffects of multiple wars and Trump’s slew of tariffs, among other challenges; yet, it worked towards sustainability and inclusive development, effectively steering the group through the calamity; India’s chapter is set to continue on this path

items.title: Turbulent South America builds up resistance to U.S.’s ‘Donroe doctrine’
items.summary: Following the U.S military’s capture of Venezuelan President Maduro, the continent of more than 450 million people is confronting the reassertion o fa Monroe Doctrine–style logic treating it as a zone of U.S. influence; however internal divisions undermine the region’s ability to form a united front

items.title: With Bolsonaro in jail, Brazil’s right in disarray as Trump warms up to Lula
items.summary: The former President’s absence has done little to rally his supporters even as his family is embroiled in a bitter succession battle; the markets and U.S. officials signal waning support for the Bolsonaro brand; with legal troubles and low public backing, the far-right in Brazil faces a turbulent path ahead

items.title: Trump’s ‘Spear of the South’ sparks regional alarm as Venezuela braces for U.S. moves
items.summary: Washington declares operation ‘Spear of the South’ against ‘narco-terrorists’ and ‘cartel head’ Nicolas Maduro. As it moves heavy firepower into the Caribbean, observers say the move could be aimed at regime change in Caracas and reasserting the U.S.’s power over the region amid rising influence of China and Russia

items.title: The South at the centre
items.summary: As the U.S. withdraws and Europe falters, the Global South, led by Brazil, steps up with bold initiatives linking climate goals to economic growth

items.title: After the gunfire: How Rio’s deadliest police raid exploded into a political battlefield
items.summary: While Rio Governor hailed the operation as a victory against crime, hundreds of favela residents have marched through the alleys, carrying photos of the dead and chanting, ‘It wasn’t a war, it was a massacre’; Lula’s allies see the raid as an attempt to shift the spotlight to his alleged softness on crime

items.title: Trump’s boycott and Europe’s backslide may make BRICS lead climate debate and action at COP30
items.summary: The absence of the world’s largest emitter — historically — and biggest economy from COP30 may seem like a setback, yet Brazilian analysts believe the summit will move forward without Washington

items.title: United in fear, divided in politics – a view from Narva, an Estonian city bordering Russia
items.summary: Some 201 km away from capital Tallinn, Narva is the most Russian speaking town of Europe with over 96% Russian speakers among its roughly 53,000 residents.

items.title: Watch: Trump should end the war and ask Israel to leave Iran alone | The Hindu Editorial
items.summary: Donald Trump, whose administration has already eased some sanctions on Iran’s oil, could be looking for a deal, and Tehran could reciprocate if common ground is found. But for any diplomatic effort to progress, Mr. Trump must first rein in his ally Israel, which continues to pound Iran and Lebanon like an unhinged bully.
